title: pharnaceum mollugo
intro: Pharnaceum mollugo, also known as Bugleweed or Lycopus europaeus, is a perennial herb in the mint family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include acting as a cardiac depressant and having anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Pharnaceum mollugo include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce anxiety and stress, and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. Pharnaceum mollugo can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often combined with other herbs in herbal preparations for its purported medicinal benefits.
